passage: A consumer research firm tracked monthly smartphone sales for three major brands during the first quarter of 2024. The firm reported that Brand X outsold both Brand Y and Brand Z in every month of the quarter.
stem: Which choice most effectively uses data from the figure to support the firm's report?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Brand X sold 2.4 million units in January, 2.7 million in February, and 3.1 million in March, while Brand Y's highest monthly total was 1.9 million and Brand Z's was 1.6 million.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Brand Y sold 1.7 million units in January, 1.8 million in February, and 1.9 million in March, showing steady growth throughout the quarter.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Brand Z sold 1.6 million units in March, which was higher than its January total of 1.2 million units.

choices.label: D
choices.text: In March, Brand X sold 3.1 million units, Brand Y sold 1.9 million units, and Brand Z sold 1.6 million units.

explanation: The claim is that Brand X outsold both competitors in every month. Choice A supports this by showing Brand X's monthly totals (2.4M, 2.7M, 3.1M) all exceed the highest totals of Brand Y (1.9M) and Brand Z (1.6M). Choice B discusses only Brand Y's growth pattern. Choice C discusses only Brand Z. Choice D only shows March data, which doesn't demonstrate that Brand X led in every month.

distractorRationale.A: Correct: cites Brand X's sales for all three months and shows they exceed the peak sales of both competitors.
distractorRationale.B: Wrong (irrelevant data): discusses Brand Y's growth trend but does not compare Brand Y to Brand X.
distractorRationale.C: Wrong (misread value): compares Brand Z's January and March figures but does not address the claim about Brand X outselling competitors.
distractorRationale.D: Wrong (incomplete evidence): shows only one month (March) where Brand X leads, but the claim asserts Brand X led in every month of the quarter.

imageDescription: Line graph titled 'Monthly Smartphone Sales by Brand, Q1 2024.' X-axis: Month (January, February, March). Y-axis: Units Sold (millions), range 0-3.5. Three lines: Brand X: 2.4, 2.7, 3.1. Brand Y: 1.7, 1.8, 1.9. Brand Z: 1.2, 1.4, 1.6. Source: Consumer Research Firm, 2024.

chartData.title: Monthly Smartphone Sales by Brand, Q1 2024

passageMetadata.source: Adapted from Global Consumer Research Firm quarterly sales report
